question.text: Currency futures in India were first launched in which year and on which currency pair?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Currency derivatives trading was introduced with the launch of currency futures in the USD-INR pair in 2008. NSE launched it on August 29, 2008, followed by BSE on October 1, 2008, and MSEI (then MCX-SX) on October 7, 2008. **The Trap:** Cross-currency pairs (EUR-USD, GBP-USD, USD-JPY) were introduced later, not at launch. Students may confuse the launch year with other market developments. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I (Currency Derivatives) covers this history in detail, along with contract specifications for all currency pairs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Currency futures launch timeline: 2008 (NSE → BSE → MSEI). Currently: 4 INR pairs (USD, EUR, GBP, JPY) + 3 cross-currency pairs (EUR-USD, GBP-USD, USD-JPY).
